bio.quote: High King Harald is the name tied to the old unification: the Danelaw that bound feuding clans into one realm. His word still echoes in writs, stamped notices, and the obligations of a Konungs-ferð.In

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

novice.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

novice.description: Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

system.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

novice.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

system.description:
Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

novice.description:

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

system.description:
Skills: Vigilant, Strong, Persuasive.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 50

Perk: *Take the Hit*. Once per encounter, when an ally nearby would suffer the consequences of a hazard or complication, you can step in to take it instead.

novice.description:

Languages: No additional languages.

system.description: Whenever you are in a natural environment (but not in a settlement in that environment), you gain an advantage (second chance) on tests made using the Alertness skill, and you can’t be surprised. Additionally, you have a connection to nature that warns you if any natural disaster is imminent within the next 72 hours, though you don’t know exactly what it will entail (an earthquake, a wildfire, and so forth).

novice.description: Whenever you are in a natural environment (but not in a settlement in that environment), you gain an advantage (second chance) on tests made using the Alertness skill, and you can’t be surprised. Additionally, you have a connection to nature that warns you if any natural disaster is imminent within the next 72 hours, though you don’t know exactly what it will entail (an earthquake, a wildfire, and so forth).

system.description:
Strategist. Defender. Leader. With weapon in hand, you lead allies into the maw of battle, barking out commands that inspire your fellow heroes to move faster and strike more precisely. All the while, you stand between your compatriots and death, taunting the followers of evil to best you if they can.

As a tactician, you have abilities that heal your allies and grant them increased damage, movement, and attacks, even as you leave your enemies struggling to respond.

system.description: Your expertise with weapons has grown. Whenever you use a signature ability from one of your equipped kits or make a Free Attack using a weapon from one of your equipped kits, you gain an advantage (second chance).

novice.description: Your expertise with weapons has grown. Whenever you use a signature ability from one of your equipped kits or make a Free Attack using a weapon from one of your equipped kits, you gain an advantage (second chance).

system.description: The first time each combat round that you or any ally damages a target marked by you, you gain 2 focus instead of 1.

novice.description: The first time each combat round that you or any ally damages a target marked by you, you gain 2 focus instead of 1.

system.description: When you gain focus at the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 3 focus instead of 2.

novice.description: When you gain focus at the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 3 focus instead of 2.

system.description:
If you are not surprised when combat begins, your side gets to go first.

If an enemy has an ability that allows their side to go first, you roll as usual to determine who goes first.

novice.description:
If you are not surprised when combat begins, your side gets to go first.

If an enemy has an ability that allows their side to go first, you roll as usual to determine who goes first.

system.description:
You have drilled with a broad array of arms and armor, and have developed techniques to optimize their use. You can use and gain the benefits of two kits, including both their signature abilities. Whenever you would choose or change one kit, you can choose or change your second kit as well. See Chapter 6: Kits for more information. (Quick Build: Shining Armor, Sniper.)

If both kits grant you the same benefit, you take one or the other and can’t change your choice until you finish a extended rest. (This usually means taking the higher of two bonuses.)

For example, if you take the Shining Armor and Sniper kits, you gain the following benefits overall:

  • Toughness Bonus: +12 per echelon

  • Resistance Bonus: +1

  • Melee Damage Bonus: +2/+2/+2

  • Ranged Damage Bonus: +0/+0/+4

  • Speed Bonus: +1

  • Ranged Distance Bonus: +10

  • Disengage Bonus: +1

  • You can use the Patient Shot and Protective Attack signature abilities.

Kit signature abilities have their kit’s bonuses already applied, which might require you to adjust the bonuses of the signature abilities you gain from a kit. For example, you might take the Martial Artist kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +2/+2/+2, and the Mountain kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +0/+0/+4. If you choose to use the Mountain kit’s damage bonus, then the Battle Grace signature ability from the Martial Artist kit loses the +2/+2/+2 bonus from that kit, reducing its usual 5/8/11 damage for its tier 1, tier 2, and tier 3 outcomes to 3/6/9. It then gains the +0/+0/+4 of the Mountain kit to deal 3/6/13 damage.

system.description: The Guisarmier kit is for those who want to use a polearm for extended reach while remaining protected by sturdy armor. This is the kit that allows you to become the ultimate halberd, longspear, or glaive fighter

novice.description: The Guisarmier kit is for those who want to use a polearm for extended reach while remaining protected by sturdy armor. This is the kit that allows you to become the ultimate halberd, longspear, or glaive fighter

system.description: The Spellsword kit combines melee strikes and a little bit of magic, letting you create a warrior who doesn’t have to choose between the incantation and the blade.

novice.description: The Spellsword kit combines melee strikes and a little bit of magic, letting you create a warrior who doesn’t have to choose between the incantation and the blade.

system.description:
The ring of steel panics others but brings order to your mind, granting you a Heroic Resource called focus.

Focus in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain focus equal to your Milestones. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 focus.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or any ally damages a creature marked by you (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.zyd6C7phOZr6EAQ9]{Mark}), you gain 1 focus. The first time in a combat round that any ally within 10 meters of you uses a heroic ability, you gain 1 focus.

You lose any remaining focus at the end of the encounter.

Focus Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain focus outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost focus without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs focus, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited focus on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of focus equal to your Milestones.

system.description: Your expertise with weapons has grown to true mastery. Whenever you use a signature ability from one of your equipped kits or make a Free Attack using a weapon from one of your equipped kits, you can negate a disadvantage (second chance) on the attribute test or reduce a double disadvantage (second chance) to a disadvantage (second chance).

novice.description: Your expertise with weapons has grown to true mastery. Whenever you use a signature ability from one of your equipped kits or make a Free Attack using a weapon from one of your equipped kits, you can negate a disadvantage (second chance) on the attribute test or reduce a double disadvantage (second chance) to a disadvantage (second chance).

system.description: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics. At the start of an encounter, you can use a free triggered action to use your Mark ability against one enemy you have line of effect to, even if you are surprised. You can then slide the marked target up to 3 meters, ignoring resistance. The target can’t be moved in a way that would harm them (such as over a cliff), leave them dying, or result in them suffering a condition or other negative effect.

novice.description: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics. At the start of an encounter, you can use a free triggered action to use your Mark ability against one enemy you have line of effect to, even if you are surprised. You can then slide the marked target up to 3 meters, ignoring resistance. The target can’t be moved in a way that would harm them (such as over a cliff), leave them dying, or result in them suffering a condition or other negative effect.

system.description: Whenever a willing ally falls and would land on you or adjacent to you, you can safely catch them as a free triggered action. Neither of you takes damage from the ally’s fall.

novice.description: Whenever a willing ally falls and would land on you or adjacent to you, you can safely catch them as a free triggered action. Neither of you takes damage from the ally’s fall.

system.description: On any day when you don’t take a extended rest, you can spend 1 uninterrupted hour working on a research project using a skill you have from the lore skill group. If you do so, you gain 1d10 project points toward that project.

novice.description: On any day when you don’t take a extended rest, you can spend 1 uninterrupted hour working on a research project using a skill you have from the lore skill group. If you do so, you gain 1d10 project points toward that project.

system.description: Whenever you speak one-on-one with a creature, you can ask them one question about themself that might typically offend them or raise suspicion. If they choose not to answer honestly, they simply deflect or redirect the question, with no further complications. If they choose to answer honestly, the creature can immediately ask you a question about yourself in turn, which you must answer honestly.

novice.description: Whenever you speak one-on-one with a creature, you can ask them one question about themself that might typically offend them or raise suspicion. If they choose not to answer honestly, they simply deflect or redirect the question, with no further complications. If they choose to answer honestly, the creature can immediately ask you a question about yourself in turn, which you must answer honestly.

system.description: Doing your duty, playing fair, and dying honorably in battle is your opponent’s job. You’ll do whatever it takes to keep your allies alive. You gain a skill from the intrigue skill group.

novice.description: Doing your duty, playing fair, and dying honorably in battle is your opponent’s job. You’ll do whatever it takes to keep your allies alive. You gain a skill from the intrigue skill group.

system.description:
While in your presence or working according to your plans, each of your allies gains an advantage (second chance) on tests using any skill from the intrigue skill group. Additionally, you can use the Lead skill to assist another creature with any test made using a skill from the intrigue group.

At the Director’s discretion, you and your allies can use skills from the intrigue skill group to attempt research or reconnaissance during a negotiation instead of outside of a negotiation.

novice.description:
While in your presence or working according to your plans, each of your allies gains an advantage (second chance) on tests using any skill from the intrigue skill group. Additionally, you can use the Lead skill to assist another creature with any test made using a skill from the intrigue group.

At the Director’s discretion, you and your allies can use skills from the intrigue skill group to attempt research or reconnaissance during a negotiation instead of outside of a negotiation.

system.description: You have trained your squad to work together, stay silent, and wait for the opportune time to strike. Whenever you or any ally within 10 meters of you becomes hidden, that creature gains 1 surge.

novice.description: You have trained your squad to work together, stay silent, and wait for the opportune time to strike. Whenever you or any ally within 10 meters of you becomes hidden, that creature gains 1 surge.

system.description: You have mastered the ability to distract your foes, allowing you and your allies to take second chance (two rolls, take best) of their gaps in attention. Whenever you or any ally attempts to hide, any creature marked by you doesn’t count as an observer. Additionally, you and your allies can use other allies as cover for the purpose of hiding.

novice.description: You have mastered the ability to distract your foes, allowing you and your allies to take second chance (two rolls, take best) of their gaps in attention. Whenever you or any ally attempts to hide, any creature marked by you doesn’t count as an observer. Additionally, you and your allies can use other allies as cover for the purpose of hiding.

system.description: You and any ally within 10 meters of you can move at full speed while sneaking. Additionally, enemies within 10 meters of you take a disadvantage (second chance) on tests made to search for you or your allies while any of you are hidden.

novice.description: You and any ally within 10 meters of you can move at full speed while sneaking. Additionally, enemies within 10 meters of you take a disadvantage (second chance) on tests made to search for you or your allies while any of you are hidden.

system.description: You have advanced your skills in subterfuge, now directing full battlefield strategy and logistics. During a montage test or negotiation, you can obtain one automatic success on a test made using a skill from the intrigue skill group. Additionally, you can use skills from the intrigue skill group to conceal large groups of people, such as escaping civilians and groups of guerilla warriors.

novice.description: You have advanced your skills in subterfuge, now directing full battlefield strategy and logistics.
